checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture defines the interviewer, core/layer document, and two-mode checker boundaries and their interactions; ## Components identifies integration with existing skills, templates, checkers, tests, review prechecks, and metadata and gives the relevant Markdown/Mermaid, POSIX shell, PowerShell, and Python technology choices. No issues found.
SKIP: no explicit component dependency declarations found in ## Architecture or ## Components; checked the architecture narrative for circular import patterns and found none.

design.md ## Data Plan explicitly identifies modified document schemas, affected Markdown templates/manifests/metadata/fixtures, and states that no database migration is required with a repository-mode compatibility rationale. No issues found.

design.md ## API / Contract Plan explicitly states that no product endpoint, RPC, or event is added, deprecated, or breaking-changed, and identifies the optional backward-compatible checker CLI as the sole changed public contract; ## Structure Checker Interface defines its POSIX and PowerShell inputs, required inventory, diagnostics, and exit behavior. No issues found.

design.md ## Security Boundaries names the feature-selector-to-filesystem and visual-input-to-documentation trust boundaries and supplies an auth/authz mechanism, data classification, and relevant OWASP concern for each. No issues found.

design.md ## Test Strategy covers unit/static validation scope and mocking, integration boundaries and tamper/path fixtures, acceptance mapping from TEST-001 through TEST-019 to AC-001 through AC-016, and concrete evidence through tests/scripts.tests.ps1, scoped suites, the complete repository suite, and retained repository test-run output. No issues found.

Components referenced by the architecture and contract plan are defined in design.md ## Components, ## Layer Artifact Model, ## Structure Checker Interface, or are established artifacts and workflow elements identified by requirements.md, including contracts, review-loop prechecks, and sdd-sudo. No issues found.

design.md ## Architecture Decision Records explicitly states that no repository-wide ADR is required because the additive plugin change preserves review-gate ownership and the two-command workflow; the design introduces no new technology or architectural departure requiring an ADR. No issues found.
